_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_help' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Discover tools, search capabilities, and fetch detailed parameters, examples, and workflows. Use this first when you are unsure which Burp MCP tool fits.

capability.description: What you want to do (e.g., 'scan for vulnerabilities', 'decode data') - finds matching tools

list.description: Set to true to list all available tools with summaries

section.description: When requesting specific tool: which section (default: full)

tool.description: Specific tool name (e.g., 'burp_scanner') - returns full documentation for that tool

tools.name: burp_help

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_help. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

items.description: Array result when a tool returns a top-level list.

limitChars.description: Bridge result-size limit used for truncation.

originalChars.description: Serialized result size before bridge truncation.

text.description: Human-readable response when verbose output is requested.

truncated.description: True when the bridge replaced an oversized structured result with a truncation summary.

tools.title: Help & Discovery

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_proxy_history' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Read chronological HTTP traffic captured through Burp's proxy. Use list, detail, or iterate; use burp_sitemap_analysis for deduplicated site-wide structure.

action.description: Mode: list (URLs), detail (specific entries), iterate (sequential full details)

afterEntry.description: Only return entries with ID strictly greater than this value

afterTime.description: Filter requests after this time (ISO-8601 or relative like '1h', '30m', '7d')

beforeEntry.description: Only return entries with ID strictly less than this value

beforeTime.description: Filter requests before this time (ISO-8601 format)

contentType.description: Filter by content type (JSON, XML, URL_ENCODED, MULTIPART, AMF)

count.description: Number of entries to return in iterate mode (default: 1)

limit.description: Maximum entries for list mode (default: 100, no max limit)

maxBodyBytes.description: Max bytes of response body to return inline (when showBody=true). Default 5000 — keeps context small. Set 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large HTML responses can blow up the context window). When the cap kicks in, bodyTruncatedBytes reports the number of dropped bytes.

modifiedOnly.description: Show only requests that were modified

parameterType.description: Parameter location (URL, BODY, COOKIE, JSON, XML, MULTIPART)

path.description: Filter by URL path - partial match

verbose.description: Return decorated markdown output (for human debugging). Default returns compact JSON to save tokens.

tools.name: burp_proxy_history

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_proxy_history. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

status.description: HTTP status code (null if no response)

items.description: Array result when a tool returns a top-level list.

limitChars.description: Bridge result-size limit used for truncation.

originalChars.description: Serialized result size before bridge truncation.

text.description: Human-readable response when verbose output is requested.

truncated.description: True when the bridge replaced an oversized structured result with a truncation summary.

tools.description: Create Burp Repeater UI tabs for manual testing only. This tool does not send requests; use burp_custom_http for programmatic HTTP.

proxyUrl.description: URL from proxy history to send (for SEND_FROM_PROXY)

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_proxy_interceptor' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Manually hold, inspect, modify, forward, or drop browser proxy traffic. Scope enable with filters and trigger held requests non-blocking.

filter_host.description: enable only: hold only requests whose host contains this substring.

filter_method.description: enable only: hold only requests with this HTTP method (e.g. "POST"). Case-insensitive.

filter_path.description: enable only: hold requests whose URL contains this substring (e.g. "/cart"). With filter_regex=true, treated as a regex matched against the full URL.

filter_regex.description: enable only: treat filter_path as a regex (matched against the full URL) instead of a substring. Default false.

modifications.description: Fields to change before forwarding. Request actions use method/path/body/headers; response actions also support status_code and reason_phrase.

options.description: Optional request handling behavior for modify_request.

drop.description: Drop instead of forwarding the request.

intercept_ui.description: Also show the request in Burp's UI intercept view.

request_id.description: Request ID from get_queue. Legacy alias for response_id on response actions.

response_id.description: Response ID from get_response_queue for response actions.

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

tools.name: burp_proxy_interceptor

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_proxy_interceptor. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

limitChars.description: Bridge result-size limit used for truncation.

originalChars.description: Serialized result size before bridge truncation.

text.description: Human-readable response when verbose output is requested.

_meta.anthropic/searchHint: scan vulnerabilities active passive crawl audit
_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_scanner' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Start Burp scans and crawls, track progress, retrieve issues, import BChecks, and scan raw requests with targeted insertion points.

action.description: Scanner action. START_SCAN: scan URLs from scratch (urls[]). SCAN_SPECIFIC_REQUEST: scan one raw request (use insertionPointParams/Values for targeted scanning). ADD_TO_SCAN: add to existing scan (scanId). CRAWL_ONLY: discover URLs without scanning. GET_STATUS/GET_ISSUES/CANCEL_SCAN/LIST_SCANS: track active scans. FILTER_ISSUES/SCAN_METRICS/CLEAR_ISSUES: manage results. GENERATE_REPORT: HTML/XML export. IMPORT_BCHECK: load custom check definition.

config.description: Scan configuration: LIGHT, MEDIUM, or THOROUGH

cookies.description: Cookies to include as name/value pairs.

headers.description: Additional HTTP headers to include in scan requests.

host.description: Target host (for SCAN_SPECIFIC_REQUEST, or ADD_TO_SCAN with raw request). If omitted, parsed from Host header.

insertionPointParams.description: Parameter names to scan - auto-finds their values and creates insertion points. Works with URL query params (name=value), form body params, and JSON values ("name": "value"). E.g. ["username", "password"] finds the values of those parameters.

insertionPointValues.description: Values to scan in the request - auto-finds byte offsets. E.g. ["admin", "secret"] finds those strings in the request and creates insertion points for them.

issueIndex.description: 1-based issue indices for detail mode (e.g., [1, 3, 5]). Omit for summary mode.

limit.description: Max issues to return in summary mode (default: 50)

offset.description: Number of issues to skip in summary mode (default: 0)

port.description: Target port. If omitted, parsed from Host header (default: 443 for HTTPS, 80 for HTTP)

request.description: HTTP request to add to scan. IMPORTANT: include port in Host header (example.com:443 for HTTPS, example.com:80 for HTTP) — without explicit port, parsing falls back to defaults that may not match useHttps.

urlFilter.description: URL pattern filter with wildcards (for FILTER_ISSUES/GET_ISSUES)

useHttps.description: Required for SCAN_SPECIFIC_REQUEST and ADD_TO_SCAN actions. true=HTTPS:443, false=HTTP:80 (port can be overridden via 'port' parameter or Host header). If omitted, parsing falls back to URL/Host header — explicit is safer.

useProxySession.description: Automatically use authenticated session from proxy history (default: true). Finds the most recent valid request to the target URL and extracts all headers/cookies. Set to false to use basic unauthenticated requests.

verbose.description: Return decorated markdown (for human debugging). Default returns compact JSON.

tools.name: burp_scanner

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_scanner. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

insertionPointsResolved.description: Number of insertion points resolved for a specific-request scan

issuesFound.description: Number of issues found so far

items.description: Array result when a tool returns a top-level list.

limitChars.description: Bridge result-size limit used for truncation.

originalChars.description: Serialized result size before bridge truncation.

requestCount.description: Number of requests processed by the scan

text.description: Human-readable response when verbose output is requested.

truncated.description: True when the bridge replaced an oversized structured result with a truncation summary.

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_add_issue' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Create custom Burp audit issues with evidence, severity, remediation, and optional proxy-history-derived request/response context.

background.description: Technical background about this vulnerability type (AI can provide context)

detail.description: Detailed description of this specific instance, including vulnerable parameters, payloads used, etc.

evidence.description: Specific evidence for this instance (request/response snippets, error messages, etc.)

filters.description: Proxy history filters used to locate evidence requests.

contains.description: Text to search for in request or response.

regex.description: Regex to search for in request or response.

issueType.description: Vulnerability type (e.g., 'SQL injection', 'Cross-site scripting'). The tool will automatically group with similar existing issues.

name.description: Specific issue instance description (optional - will be added to detail if provided)

payload.description: The actual payload used in the attack (will be bolded in detail)

references.description: References and links for further reading (OWASP, CWE, etc.)

remediation.description: Remediation guidance - how to fix this vulnerability (AI can generate this)

request.description: Single raw HTTP request as evidence (alternative to 'requests' array). Use 'request' for one evidence pair, 'requests' for multiple. IMPORTANT: include port in Host header (example.com:443 for HTTPS, example.com:80 for HTTP) — without explicit port, parsing falls back to defaults that may not match useHttps.

requests.description:
Evidence requests (array). Use 'requests' for multiple evidence pairs, 'request' for one. Each entry can be a raw request string or an object (e.g. {"raw": "..."}).
Objects may also include metadata fields like 'id' or 'notes' – only 'raw'/'request' are required to build evidence. IMPORTANT: include port in Host header (example.com:443 for HTTPS, example.com:80 for HTTP) — without explicit port, parsing falls back to defaults that may not match useHttps.

response.description: Single raw HTTP response as evidence (alternative to 'responses' array). Use 'response' for one evidence pair, 'responses' for multiple.

responses.description: Evidence responses matching the requests array. Accepts raw strings or objects with a 'raw'/'response'/'content'/'body' field.

url.description: URL where the issue was found

tools.description: Manage tokens, Burp's cookie jar, session validation, and automatic session handling actions.

autoRefresh.description: Enable automatic token refresh on 401/403 responses

expiration.description: Cookie expiration in ISO-8601 format (for COOKIE_JAR_SET, optional)

extractPattern.description: Regex pattern to extract tokens (for EXTRACT_TOKENS)

tokenName.description: Token/cookie name — used for both SET_TOKEN (in-memory token store) and COOKIE_JAR_SET/COOKIE_JAR_DELETE (Burp's native cookie jar).

tokenValue.description: Token/cookie value — used for both SET_TOKEN (in-memory token store) and COOKIE_JAR_SET (Burp's native cookie jar).

url.description: URL for session operations (required for some actions)

validityKeywords.description: Keywords to check for session validity

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_scope' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: View, add, remove, check, and analyze Burp target scope, including bulk operations.

includeSubdomains.description: When adding to scope, also include ALL subdomains — the equivalent of Burp's UI 'Include subdomains' checkbox. Implemented via an advanced-scope host regex (^(?:.*\.)?host$) injected through project-options import, since Montoya's scope API can't express wildcards. Default true. Set false to add only the exact host/URL. An explicit '*.host' url also triggers this.

url.description: URL or hostname to add/remove/check. For 'add' action: Can be a full URL (https://example.com) or just a hostname (example.com) which will add all protocols and subdomains

urls.description: URL array for bulk_add and bulk_check actions only.

verbose.description: Return decorated markdown (for human debugging). Default returns compact JSON.

_meta.anthropic/searchHint: burp settings project user options import export json scope proxy config
_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_config' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Read and write Burp project or user options JSON for settings not covered by typed tools.

action.description: Config action. RESET_PROJECT_OPTIONS restores a known-default for supported 'path' values (currently 'target.scope' → empty scope); Burp has no generic reset, so other paths must be reset by SETting an explicit default.

json.description: SET only (required): the options JSON to import, in the same full nested shape GET returns (e.g. {"target":{"scope":{...}}}).

path.description: Used by GET (optional) and REQUIRED by RESET_PROJECT_OPTIONS; ignored by SET. Dotted options path, rooted like the JSON — PROJECT roots: 'target', 'proxy', 'project_options' (e.g. 'target.scope', 'proxy', 'project_options.connections'); USER: 'user_options.*' (e.g. 'user_options.proxy'). On GET, omit to export the FULL config (troubleshooting/backup; large); a path that matches no key falls through to the full export. On RESET, must be a supported default path (currently 'target.scope').

verbose.description: Return decorated markdown instead of compact JSON.

fromProxy.description: Send from proxy history instead of creating new request (for SEND_TO_ORGANIZER)

itemId.description: Item ID for GET_ITEM_BY_ID or GET_ITEM_STATUS

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return (for LIST_ITEMS operations, default 20)

methodFilter.description: HTTP method to filter by (for LIST_ITEMS_FILTERED)

url.description: URL to send to organizer or filter by (for SEND_TO_ORGANIZER and LIST_ITEMS_FILTERED)

urlPattern.description: URL pattern to filter (supports partial matching for LIST_ITEMS_FILTERED)

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_annotate' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Add notes and highlights across Burp data sources, search annotations, and manage auto-annotation rules.

autoRules.description: Auto-annotation rules applied to matching proxy request URLs.

notes.description: Notes to apply to matching requests.

entryId.description: Exact entry id (1-based). For ANNOTATE_PROXY / CLEAR_ANNOTATIONS source=PROXY: same id space as burp_proxy_history returns (proxy-history index + 1). For ANNOTATE_TARGET: index into api.siteMap().requestResponses() + 1 — Burp's Montoya sitemap exposes no stable id, so this is the current in-memory ordering and is NOT interchangeable with the proxy id. For CLEAR_ANNOTATIONS the source param must be PROXY when entryId is given (or omitted, which defaults to PROXY); TARGET+entryId or ALL+entryId are rejected. url/method/pattern filters are ignored when entryId is set.

method.description: Filter by HTTP method (case-insensitive). Honored by ANNOTATE_PROXY, ANNOTATE_TARGET, CLEAR_ANNOTATIONS, GET_ANNOTATIONS — when present, only entries whose request method matches are touched.

notes.description: Notes to add to the item

notesMode.description: How to combine the new note with any existing one. APPEND (default) — concatenate with \n\n separator; REPLACE — overwrite existing notes.

pattern.description: Pattern to match URLs for bulk annotation

source.description: Source component. Values: PROXY|TARGET|ORGANIZER|REPEATER|INTRUDER|SCANNER|WEBSOCKET|COLLABORATOR|ALL. Note: ANNOTATE_* actions infer source from the action name; only GET_ANNOTATIONS, SEARCH_BY_ANNOTATION, CLEAR_ANNOTATIONS need 'source' explicitly.

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

location.description: Where to apply the Bambda (default: PROXY_HTTP_HISTORY)

_meta.anthropic/searchHint: inject auth header match replace rules auto-modify browser playwright scanner traffic
_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_global_interceptor' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Automatically apply auth, headers, match/replace rules, and rate limits across traffic from all Burp tools.

rule.description: Rule config used by add_request_rule/add_response_rule/add_websocket_rule. HTTP rules match url_pattern/method/headers/status and modify headers, body, method, path, or status. WebSocket rules use match_pattern/replace_text/direction/drop.

body_search.description: Body text or regex to replace.

match_pattern.description: WebSocket payload text or regex to match.

method.description: HTTP method to match for request rules.

required_headers.description: Headers that must already be present for a request rule to match.

status_code.description: Exact response status code to match.

status_range.description: Response status range to match, for example 400-499.

url_pattern.description: URL substring or regex to match for HTTP rules.

use_regex.description: Treat url_pattern or match_pattern as a regex.

rules_data.description: Object returned by export_rules and accepted by import_rules.

tools.description: Burp tool sources allowed to pass through the global interceptor.

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

tools.name: burp_global_interceptor

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_global_interceptor. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

_meta.anthropic/searchHint: send raw HTTP request modify replay fuzz
_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_custom_http' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Primary programmatic HTTP client for single, parallel, and pipelined requests, protocol analysis, and raw request work.

action.description: The HTTP action to perform. SEND_REQUEST sends a single request, SEND_PARALLEL sends multiple for race conditions, TOGGLE_REQUEST_METHOD converts GET↔POST, ANALYZE_PROTOCOL checks HTTP/HTTPS configuration

add_to_sitemap.description: Add the request/response to Burp's Site Map for visibility and further testing. This makes requests from burp_custom_http visible in Target > Site Map. Default: true

allow_h2c.description: Allow HTTP/2 cleartext (h2c) mode for non-TLS connections. When true, attempts HTTP/2 without TLS. Note: Server must support h2c protocol upgrade. Most servers only support HTTP/2 over TLS

connection_id.description: Unique identifier for connection reuse. Requests with the same connection_id will use the same TCP connection. Useful for maintaining session state or testing connection-specific behaviors

expect_responses.description: SEND_PIPELINED only. Number of HTTP responses to wait for before closing the socket. Defaults to requests.length. Override when smuggling shapes consume requests asymmetrically (e.g. the smuggled request may be absorbed by the back-end queue and produce no client-visible response). Range: 0-50.

follow_redirects.description: ⚠️ DEPRECATED — DO NOT USE. Use redirection_mode instead. [DEPRECATED - use redirection_mode instead] Legacy boolean for redirect following. true maps to ALWAYS, false maps to NEVER. Maintained for backward compatibility

http_mode.description: HTTP protocol version. HTTP_1 for standard HTTP/1.1, HTTP_2 for multiplexed HTTP/2, HTTP_2_IGNORE_ALPN for HTTP/2 without ALPN negotiation (useful for non-standard implementations), AUTO lets Burp choose the best protocol

inter_request_delay_ms.description: SEND_PIPELINED only. Gap (ms) between writing successive requests to the SAME socket. 0 (default) = concatenate all bytes and send in a single write() — required for most smuggling attacks. >0 = write each request, sleep, write next — used for pause-based desync and timing-sensitive attacks. Range: 0-10000.

max_body_bytes.description: Max bytes of the response body to return inline. Default 5000 (keeps context small). Set 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large HTML pages can blow up the context window). Set any positive int to override the cap. body_length always reflects the true size; if the body was truncated, body_truncated_bytes reports how many bytes were dropped.

max_concurrency.description: SEND_PARALLEL only. Maximum number of in-flight requests at any moment. Requests beyond this wait in a FIFO queue. Default 10 (bug-fix: previously unbounded, which caused tail requests to be silently dropped as 'No response' on large batches). Range: 1-50. Set to 50 to opt back into burst/fire-all-at-once behavior for race-condition testing.

proxy_host.description: Hostname or IP of Burp's proxy listener. Used only when route_via_proxy=true. Default: 127.0.0.1.

proxy_port.description: Port of Burp's proxy listener. Used only when route_via_proxy=true. Default: 8080. Range: 1-65535.

raw_request.description: Send the request bytes verbatim: no request-line rewriting (absolute-URI form preserved), no URL re-parsing, no implicit Host-header insertion, no header reordering. Line endings are still normalized LF->CRLF. Required for parser-discrepancy / request-smuggling tests where front-end and back-end disagree on how to interpret 'GET https://victim.com/admin HTTP/1.1' vs the Host header. Default false (current parsing behavior preserved).

read_timeout_ms.description: SEND_PIPELINED only. After expect_responses have been parsed, wait this many ms for any trailing bytes (stray queued responses, poisoned response from previous victim) before closing. Also serves as the per-read socket timeout during the main read loop. Range: 100-60000.

redirection_mode.description: How to handle HTTP redirects. ALWAYS follows all redirects, NEVER ignores all redirects, SAME_HOST only follows redirects to the same host (prevents redirect to external sites), IN_SCOPE only follows redirects to targets in Burp's scope

request.description:
Raw HTTP request string. Line endings auto-normalized. ALWAYS include port in Host header: 'Host: example.com:80' for HTTP, 'Host: example.com:443' for HTTPS. Example: 'GET /path HTTP/1.1
Host: example.com:80
Connection: close

'. Without port, defaults to HTTPS:443 causing timeouts on HTTP-only servers.

request_delay_ms.description: SEND_PARALLEL only. Minimum gap in milliseconds between dispatching successive requests from the queue. 0 = no pacing (fire as fast as max_concurrency allows). Useful for rate-limited targets. Range: 0-10000.

requests.description: Array of raw HTTP request strings for parallel/race condition testing. Line endings auto-normalized. ALWAYS include port in Host header: ':80' for HTTP, ':443' for HTTPS. Without port, defaults to HTTPS:443 causing timeouts on HTTP-only servers.

response_timeout.description: Maximum time to wait for a response in milliseconds. Prevents hanging on slow endpoints. Range: 100-600000 (0.1 second to 10 minutes)

route_via_proxy.description: Tunnel the request(s) through Burp's local proxy listener so they appear in Proxy → HTTP history. DEFAULTS: TRUE for SEND_REQUEST (history visibility is usually what you want), FALSE for SEND_PARALLEL and SEND_PIPELINED (proxy may serialize parallel dispatch and almost certainly breaks pipelined-smuggling framing since Burp re-frames requests on its own upstream connections). MECHANISM: opens TCP to proxy_host:proxy_port, sends CONNECT target:port, then (for HTTPS targets) wraps the tunnel in TLS, then sends the actual request bytes. Burp decrypts, logs to HTTP history, and forwards upstream. TRADE-OFFS WHEN ON: (1) http_mode is forced to HTTP/1.1 (proxy CONNECT path can't multiplex H2), (2) redirection_mode is ignored (you must follow redirects yourself), (3) connection_id is ignored, (4) Burp's match/replace and proxy interception rules apply to the request — bad for raw_request byte-exact tests. TURN OFF when: you need raw byte fidelity (smuggling labs), you're testing through a flaky/dead proxy, or you want HTTP/2 / redirects / connection reuse. TURN ON when: you want the requests visible in HTTP history for review / cross-referencing with browser traffic.

server_name_indicator.description: Server Name Indication (SNI) hostname for TLS connections. Overrides the default SNI derived from the Host header. Useful for testing virtual hosting, CDN configurations, or SNI-based routing. Example: 'admin.example.com'

target_host.description: TCP destination hostname or IP. Overrides whatever is in the Host header. Required for host-header SSRF / routing-based SSRF / virtual-host confusion attacks where the Host header must lie (e.g. Host: 192.168.0.1) while the socket still hits the real front-end. When set with server_name_indicator and an IP, the SNI is used for TLS and the IP for the socket. If unset, falls back to parsing the Host header (current behavior).

target_port.description: TCP destination port (paired with target_host). Defaults to 443 if HTTPS, else 80. Range: 1-65535.

upstream_tls_verification.description: Enable TLS certificate verification for upstream servers. When true, validates SSL/TLS certificates. Useful for testing certificate validation bypasses or connecting to servers with self-signed certificates

url.description: URL to analyze in ANALYZE_PROTOCOL action. Alternative to providing a full request. The tool will determine if it's HTTP or HTTPS, extract port information, and check for default ports. Example: 'https://example.com:8443/api'

use_cookie_jar.description: Automatically include cookies from Burp's cookie jar for the target domain. COOKIE BEHAVIOR: (1) Default true = cookies from jar are merged with request's Cookie header, ensuring requests use the same authenticated session as browser traffic through the proxy. (2) Request cookies take PRECEDENCE - if you specify 'Cookie: session=abc' and jar has 'session=xyz', YOUR 'session=abc' is used; only jar cookies with DIFFERENT names are added. (3) Set to FALSE when you want to: test with a completely different session, send unauthenticated requests, or test access control by using only cookies you explicitly specify. EXAMPLES: To use browser session = omit Cookie header (jar provides all). To override one cookie = include it in request (yours wins, jar adds others). To use completely custom cookies = set use_cookie_jar:false and specify your own Cookie header.

use_proxy_headers.description: Automatically copy headers from the most recent proxy history entry for the same host. HEADER BEHAVIOR: (1) Default true = finds the most recent browser request to the same host in proxy history and copies headers like User-Agent, Accept, Sec-Ch-*, Sec-Fetch-*, Accept-Language, Accept-Encoding, etc. (2) Request headers take PRECEDENCE - any headers you specify in your request will NOT be overwritten. (3) Cookie header is handled separately by use_cookie_jar, not copied from proxy history. (4) Set to FALSE when you want complete control over headers or are testing header-specific vulnerabilities. This ensures requests look like realistic browser traffic without manually copying headers.

tools.name: burp_custom_http

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_custom_http. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

items.description: Array result when a tool returns a top-level list.

limitChars.description: Bridge result-size limit used for truncation.

originalChars.description: Serialized result size before bridge truncation.

body_length.description: Full response body length in bytes

responses.description: Per-response summaries for SEND_PARALLEL and SEND_PIPELINED

routed_via_proxy.description: Whether the request was tunneled through Burp's proxy listener

success.description: Whether the request was sent successfully

text.description: Human-readable response when verbose output is requested.

truncated.description: True when the bridge replaced an oversized structured result with a truncation summary.

tools.title: HTTP Client (Primary)

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_logs' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Read extension logs, write diagnostic entries, raise Burp events, and clear captured logs.

action.description: The logging action to perform. GET_LOGS retrieves captured logs, WRITE_LOG writes new log entries, RAISE_EVENT creates Burp events, CLEAR_LOGS removes captured logs

category.description: Log category to filter when using GET_LOGS or CLEAR_LOGS. OUTPUT shows standard output logs, ERROR shows error logs, ALL shows both (default: ALL)

exceptionMessage.description: Exception message for ERROR level logging. Creates a throwable object for detailed error tracking. Uses logToError(Throwable) when stackTrace is not provided

level.description: Log level. For WRITE_LOG action use OUTPUT or ERROR. For RAISE_EVENT action use DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, or CRITICAL. Other combinations are rejected at runtime.

limit.description: Maximum number of log entries to return with GET_LOGS action. Helps manage large log outputs (default: 100, max: 1000)

message.description: Text message to log with WRITE_LOG or event message for RAISE_EVENT. Can be used alone or with exception details

object.description: Arbitrary JSON object to write with WRITE_LOG.

stackTrace.description: Stack trace details for exception logging. When provided with message, uses logToError(String, Throwable) for full error context with both message and exception

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

tools.name: burp_logs

outputSchema.description: Action-dependent structured result for burp_logs. Compact responses expose action-specific fields; verbose responses expose text.

items.description: Array result when a tool returns a top-level list.

limitChars.description: Bridge result-size limit used for truncation.

originalChars.description: Serialized result size before bridge truncation.

text.description: Human-readable response when verbose output is requested.

truncated.description: True when the bridge replaced an oversized structured result with a truncation summary.

tools.description: Inspect WebSocket proxy history and create, send on, list, or close extension WebSocket connections.

limit.description: Maximum number of entries to return

upgradeRequest.description: Optional HTTP upgrade request for create action. IMPORTANT: include port in Host header (example.com:443 for WSS, example.com:80 for WS).

url.description: WebSocket URL (ws:// or wss://) for create action

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

tools.description: Intercept, inspect, modify, forward, or drop WebSocket frames and manage frame filters and auto-modify rules.

filter_pattern.description: Regex pattern to match messages. For binary messages, pattern is matched against hex representation

new_payload.description: New payload for modify action. For text messages: plain text. For binary messages: Base64 encoded data

rule_name.description: Name for the auto-modify rule (text messages only)

search_pattern.description: Pattern to search for in text messages

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_response_analyzer' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Analyze individual HTTP responses for keywords, variations, reflection points, patterns, and anomalies.

caseSensitive.description: Whether pattern matching should be case sensitive

keywords.description: Keywords to search for in responses.

limit.description: Maximum number of proxy entries to analyze

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to search for in responses

proxyIds.description: Proxy history IDs to inspect for reflections.

testString.description: Optional label echoed in reflection output for caller correlation. Reflection detection inspects request values from proxy history.

topN.description: Number of top-ranked anomalous responses to return (for rank_anomalies)

urls.description: URLs to send or analyze for response variations.

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.

tools.title: Response Analyzer (Per-Response)

_meta.anthropic/searchHint: encode decode hash URL base64 convert
_meta.burp/help: Use burp_help with tool='burp_utilities' for full examples and workflows.

tools.description: Use Burp utilities for encoding, hashing, JSON operations, byte search, number conversion, and opt-in shell execution.

action.description: Utility action to perform. shell_execute: SAFE arg-list execution — REQUIRES commandArgs[] (a bare command string is rejected, never auto-downgraded to the shell path). shell_execute_dangerous: shell-interpreted single string (allows pipes/redirects but enables injection — only use when shell features are required). NOTE: both shell_execute* actions are DISABLED by default; enable with env BURP_MCP_SHELL_ENABLED=true (or -Dburp.mcp.shell.enabled=true) and reload the extension.

allowNonZeroExit.description: Allow non-zero exit codes without error (default: false)

allowTimeout.description: Silently handle timeout instead of error (default: false)

command.description: Single shell-interpreted string. Use only with shell_execute_dangerous when pipes/redirects are needed. For safe execution prefer commandArgs[] with shell_execute.

commandArgs.description: Command and arguments for safe shell_execute.

envVars.description: Environment variables passed to shell execution.

input.description: Input data for the utility function

length.description: Length of random data to generate (for random action)

searchPattern.description: Pattern to search for in bytes (string or regex)

timeout.description: Command timeout in seconds (default: 10, 0 to disable)

verbose.description: If true, returns formatted markdown with sections and emoji. Default: compact JSON for token efficiency.
